The EU Additives Database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

changes.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
changes.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and also process hygiene criteria.

Guidance documents are available for conducting shelf-life studies and sampling procedures for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods to comply with the food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

Specific limits for food additives in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against relevant regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
Requirements for allergen declaration on processed food labels, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against relevant regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
Specific contaminant limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against relevant regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.

Food categories for processed products, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified according to PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019, which is a foundational regulation for applying other specific rules.
General labelling requirements for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against relevant regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
Specific microbiological limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against relevant regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
Specific limits or guidelines for salt/sodium content in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against relevant regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
